full_text:
Development and Optimization of High-Grade Serous Ovarian Carcinoma Tissue Processing: Focus on 3D Culture of Cancer-Associated Fibroblasts

Project plan 
Proseminar 12.9.2024
Aino Elomaa

Supervisors:
Assistant Professor, Anniina Färkkilä, University of Helsinki
Doctoral Researcher, María Hincapié Otero, University of Helsinki
Professor, Ullamari Pesonen, University of Turku

High grade serous ovarian cancer (HGSC)

2

Created with BioRender.com

 Need for a deeper understanding of the HGSC’s biological complexity

Speaker notes:
most common subtype, accounting 70% of epithelial ovarian cancers
the most aggressive subtype
typically diagnosed at advanced stages with metastasis to omentum and surrounding abdominal organs. 
Omentum refers to this fatty tissue layer in the abdomen
first line treatment = surgery and chemotherapy 
despite initial response the treatment, relapse is common
around 25% of patients experience cancer recurrence within six months after treatment
highlights the urgent need for new therapeutical strategies 
this requires a deeper understanding of the HGSC’s biological complexity. 
in more detail, tumor microenvironment has gained increasing attention in its role in determining clinical outcomes

3

Tumor microenvironment (TME)

Created with BioRender.com

Speaker notes:
The TME refers to a complex and dynamic network of cellular and molecular components that supports tumor growth. 
Various cell types contribute to the TME: cancer cells, immune cells, and the nonimmune stromal cells including cancer-associated fibroblasts (CAFs) , mesothelial cells and endothelial cells. 
soluble factors like growth factors and cytokines, as well as capillaries and the extracellular matrix (ECM)
all these are essential in shaping the TME and affecting tumor dynamics

4

Created with BioRender.com

Speaker notes:
Among TME components, cancer-associated fibroblasts (CAFs) have been gaining increasing recognition with their importance in affecting the clinical outcomes

Cancer-associated fibroblasts (CAFs)

5

Created with BioRender.com

Speaker notes:
CAFs are activated fibroblasts, exhibiting distinct cellular phenotypes influenced by their surrounding microenvironment 
A highly heterogenous population within the TME, and different subtypes on CAFs has shown to have different effects within the TME
but generally
A high amounts of CAFs in epithelial ovarian cancer tumors is linked to advanced-stage disease such as the presence of lymph node and omentum metastases. 
CAFs contribute to tumor growth by interacting with cancer cells and influencing the extracellular matrix remodeling. 
Then, the synthesis and remodeling of the ECM by CAFs further promote metastasis and impact angiogenesis, 
and changes in ECM composition can affect immune cell infiltration and module immune responses
And this all contributes to poor drug delivery and therapeutic response

Need to further understand the complexity of HGSC’s TME

Developing an accurate model of the TME enables the exploration of novel therapeutic strategies

6

Speaker notes:
Therefore: 
There is a need to better understand the complexity of HGSC and TME’s effect as it contributes to patients’ clinical outcomes. 
And to be able to explore of novel therapeutic strategies for HGSC, a model that represents the TME and biological features of this cancer is needed. 
One option is patient-derived 3D cultures 

because of this, my thesis project aims to…

My project aims to:

7

My project aims to:

8

My project aims to:

9

My project aims to:

10

HGSC tissue samples

Collected as part of the prospective ONCOSYS-Ova clinical study 
Obtained from primary and interval debulking surgeries performed at Helsinki University Hospital

Informed written consent is required from each patient before sample acquisition
The use of clinical material was approved by the Ethics Committee of Women’s Clinic, University of Helsinki, and Helsinki Biobank.

11

Created with BioRender.com

Speaker notes:
before discussing the aims in more detail…. 
project is done with fresh cancer tissue samples received weekly
which will be resected from patients enrolled in a prospective ONCOSYS-OVA clinical trial at Helsinki University Hospital. 
Samples are obtained from primary and interval debulking surgeries. 
the clinical trial is proved by Ethics Committee and a informed written consent is required from all patient’s prior surgery.

CAF abundance in the sample is assessed by measuring the FAP-positive cell content.

 This will ensure that the obtained cell suspension maximize the yield of CAFs while maintaining cell viability and functionality.

12

1) Establish a standardized protocol for processing HGSC tissue samples

Created with BioRender.com

Speaker notes:
then to the aims, Firstly, I will establish a standardized protocol for processing HGSC tissue samples. 
The lab has already optimized a tissue processing protocol that preserves immune cells, but further optimization is needed to ensure successful dissociation of CAFs
 I will test various enzymatic dissociation methods, including dispase- and collagenase-based approaches, and compare their effectiveness. 
The efficiency of each method will be evaluated by assessing the proportion of fibroblast activation protein (FAP)-positive cells using flow cytometry.
This will ensure that the obtained cell suspension maximize the yield of CAFs while maintaining cell viability and functionality

2) Characterize key cell types within the HGSC TME

Profiling the cell composition of the samples by quantifying 
cancer cells
immune cells
endothelial cells
CAFs

 This allows for meaningful comparisons of cell type abundance within the samples

13

Created with BioRender.com

Speaker notes:
Then the dissociated cell suspension obtained from patient samples, will be characterized the key cell types using flow cytometry. 
I will quantify content of cancer cells, immune cells, endothelial cells, and CAFs. 
This allows for meaningful comparisons of cell type amounts within the samples

3) Enrich CAFs from HGSC patient samples

14

Created with BioRender.com

 By selectively enriching CAFs from HGSC patient samples, I will refine their culturing conditions.

Speaker notes:
Thirdly, I will enrich the CAFs from the tissue samples. This enrichment of CAFs from the patient-derived cell suspensions, will enable culturing of CAFs. which brings us to the fourth aim…

4) Compare the growth and behavior of patient-derived CAFs in different environments

15

Created with BioRender.com

 Further enhance our understanding of CAFs and optimize their culturing conditions.

Speaker notes:
which is to compare the growth and behavior of these patient-derived CAFs in different environments. I will evaluate them in two 3D matrices: human omentum-derived gel and basement membrane extract, and in traditional 2D cultures. 
growth and behavior will be monitored though light microscopy and I will quantify the growth rate and morphology of these cell cultures to be able to compare the culturing conditions
this will provided valuable insights on patient-derived CAFs culturing and I will be able to optimize the culturing conditions for CAFs and compare the growth

16

Ensure that the patient-derived cell suspension includes a representative composition of TME cell populations, with a particular emphasis on CAFs.

Establish optimized culturing condition for CAF.

The goal of this project:

Speaker notes:
all these efforts will ensure that the patient-derived cell suspension includes a representative composition of TME cell populations, and especially that the cell suspension included CAFs. 
and to establish the optimal culturing conditions for CAFs in 3D cultures

My thesis project aims to contribute to the Färkkilä laboratory's efforts to establish and validate a novel 3D tri-culture model of HGSC

17

Incorporates cancer cells, immune cells, and CAFs 
More comprehensive and accurate representation of the patient’s TME, capturing the complexity of HGSC.
This model could be utilized for high-throughput drug screening.

Created with BioRender.com

Speaker notes:
my whole thesis project contributes to a bigger goal of establishing and validating a novel 3D tri-culture model of HGSC TME 
the model will include cancer cells, immune cells and CAFs in a immuno-competent patient-derived culture (iPDC). 
this iPDC model already successfully supports growth on tumor-originated immune cells but 
By adding stromal components to an immunocompetent 3D model, we aim to develop a more advanced system that closely mimics the complexity of the in vivo HGSC environment 
This model could be utilized for high-throughput drug screening and exploration of novel treatment strategies.

Project outline

18

Speaker notes:
lastly, my project outline: started with optimization of tissue processing and characterization of TME cell types, and soon I will proceed to enrich the CAFs from patient samples and start refining the 3D culture methods

Thank you! Questions?
